body.notes: | DEFERRED until after first external user ship (not Leah). Productization prerequisite. Trajectory will change after ship.
body.title: Case study + lessons learned: Claude and Akien working together
body.result: Closed per Akien 2026-05-01: noise in current queue. Will still author this eventually but not as a tracked sprintable ticket — noted in workshop scope instead.
body.description:
Expanded version of the current working-together document. Rich with statistics, patterns, and honest assessment of what worked and what didn't.

Contents:
- How the Designer/Worker session pattern evolved
- Statistics: sessions, decisions (D001-D300+), tickets closed, tests written, lines of code, cost per outcome
- What Claude does well in this context (parallel minions, spec work, doc synthesis)
- What Claude does poorly (context loss, over-engineering, asking questions already answered)
- How Akien's prompting style changed over time and why
- The crystallizations — which ones landed, which were wrong, how we knew
- The skill/session architecture: what it replaced and what it cost
- Igor as the long-term goal: how CC fits into the arc
- Honest section: what would we do differently from day 1
- Lessons for other human/AI collaborations

Format: long-form markdown doc, probably 3000-5000 words. Statistics section requires pulling from sessions.md, decisions_log.dsb, commit history, queue.json.

Worker: claude + akien (collaborative write — needs Akien's perspective on several sections).
Epic: Claude
